commands.review_summary:
The branch passes model tool-support capability into the embedded attempt tool-construction planner and adds regression coverage so tool-disabled models skip local core/plugin tool prep.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +22. Total +28 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main computes the tool-construction plan before applying `supportsModelTools`, so a tool-disabled model can still trigger local tool-prep planning. I did not run a live local-model reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the remaining action is maintainer review/landing and any optional performance-proof decision.

target.title: fix(agents): skip tool prep when models disable tools
target.body:
## Summary

- Skip core/plugin tool construction when the active model declares `compat.supportsTools: false`.
- Keep existing raw-model and explicit no-tools behavior, but make the documented no-tools small-model fallback avoid tool-prep overhead too.
- Add planner coverage proving forced message delivery cannot re-enable tool construction for tool-disabled models.

Refs #86599.

## Verification

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed after rebasing onto `a17487bc9f6`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed on pushed SHA `783056045c4`: 1 routed Vitest shard, 22 tests, 3.67s.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed with exit 0 on SHA `2938f1ce52f` rebased on `03415bb6967`: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_58bf60682d2c`, run `run_bd3218821406`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`.
- Earlier AWS Crabbox `check:changed` also passed with exit 0: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_028972a33ed4`, run `run_43eb0ce4f35f`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: models configured with `compat.supportsTools: false` no longer pay to construct the OpenClaw tool surface before the runner discards it.

Real environment tested: focused local node-wrapper Vitest in a Codex worktree; AWS Crabbox Linux c7a.8xlarge changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 60m --ttl 120m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`.

Evidence after fix: focused planner regression passed 22 tests on pushed SHA `783056045c4`; AWS Crabbox `run_bd3218821406` completed `pnpm check:changed` with exit 0 and covered the `core`/`coreTests` lanes.

Observed result after fix: `resolveEmbeddedAttemptToolConstructionPlan` returns `constructTools: false`, `includeCoreTools: false`, and no runtime allowlist when `toolsEnabled: false`, even when message delivery would otherwise force the message tool.

What was not tested: no live Ollama/API small-model agent turn in this PR; this PR removes local tool-prep overhead for the documented no-tools fallback and does not change provider transport or model output handling. `origin/main` moved after the remote gate, so the final pushed SHA has fresh focused test/diff proof plus the prior same-patch Crabbox gate.
target.author: vincentkoc
